title: Bridge Essay: Towards an understanding of psychedelic-induced neuroplasticity.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nonordinary Rendering** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nonordinary — psychedelic decoupling, ego dissolution, DMN disruption, altered states.

**Towards an understanding of psychedelic-induced neuroplasticity.**. Neuropsychopharmacology.. PMID: 36123427. DOI: 10.1038/s41386-022-01389-z. Classic psychedelics, such as LSD, psilocybin, and the DMT-containing beverage ayahuasca, show some potential to treat depression, anxiety, and addiction. Importantly, clinical improvements can last for months or years after treatment. It has been theorized that these long-term improvements arise because psychedelics rapidly and lastingly stimulate neuroplasticity.
